zones.label: Single-Color LED
zones.summary: One fixed color — the basic indicator light
facts: Colors available: red, green, blue, yellow, white, infrared, ultraviolet
Forward voltage: 1.8–2.2 V (red/yellow) · 3.0–3.5 V (blue/white)
Typical current: 20 mA maximum; 10 mA for dim use
GPIO pins needed: 1 per LED (+ a 220 Ω–470 Ω series resistor from 3.3 V)
Colors at once: 1 (fixed color per LED)
Control: digital on/off, or PWM for brightness
MicroPython: Pin(n, Pin.OUT).value(1) or PWM(pin).duty_u16(x)
Best for: power indicators, simple blink programs, status lights

zones.label: RGB LED
zones.summary: Mix red, green, and blue for ~16 million colors
facts: Colors available: ~16 million (mix red, green, and blue channels)
Forward voltage: R ≈ 2.0 V; G ≈ 3.0 V; B ≈ 3.0 V (per channel)
Typical current: 20 mA per channel; up to 60 mA combined
GPIO pins needed: 3 (one per color channel; each needs its own resistor)
Colors at once: 1 mixed color (any combination of R/G/B)
Control: 3 independent PWM channels; duty cycle sets the color
MicroPython: three PWM objects — set duty_u16() on each
Best for: color-coded status indicators, mood lights, color-mixing experiments

zones.label: NeoPixel (WS2812B)
zones.summary: Addressable RGB pixels on a single data wire
facts: Colors available: 16,777,216 (24-bit RGB — 8 bits per channel)
Forward voltage: 5 V power; data signal works at 3.3 V
Typical current: up to 60 mA per pixel at full white (R+G+B = 20 mA each)
GPIO pins needed: 1 data pin for the entire chain; no limit on chain length
Colors at once: each pixel is independently programmable
Control: single-wire 800 kHz serial protocol (WS2812B timing)
MicroPython: from neopixel import NeoPixel; np = NeoPixel(pin, n)
Best for: addressable lighting strips, RGB rings, pixel art, color animations

zones.label: LED Matrix (8×8 + MAX7219)
zones.summary: A grid of single-color LEDs driven over SPI
facts: Colors available: single color (typically red or white)
Forward voltage: 5 V module power; SPI data at 3.3 V is OK
Typical current: ~320 mA max (all 64 LEDs on); ~40 mA typical display
GPIO pins needed: 3 SPI pins (DIN, CS, CLK); up to 8 modules cascade on the same 3 pins
Colors at once: 1 fixed color; brightness set globally per module (0–15 levels)
Control: SPI via the MAX7219 driver (row/column addressing)
MicroPython: max7219 library; matrix.pixel(x, y, 1) or matrix.text('Hi', 0, 0)
Best for: scrolling text, simple animations, score displays, clocks

quiz.question: Which LED type lets you control hundreds of pixels using just one GPIO data pin?
quiz.explanation: NeoPixels (WS2812B) chain together and are all driven from a single data pin using an 800 kHz serial protocol — the chain length is essentially unlimited.

quiz.question: Which LED type needs 3 GPIO pins and 3 PWM channels to mix any color?
quiz.explanation: An RGB LED has separate red, green, and blue channels. You drive all three with independent PWM signals (3 pins, 3 resistors) and the duty cycles blend into one mixed color.

quiz.question: Which LED type is best for displaying scrolling text and is driven over SPI?
quiz.explanation: An 8×8 LED matrix with a MAX7219 driver uses 3 SPI pins (DIN, CS, CLK) and can scroll text or show simple animations. Modules cascade for longer displays.

quiz.question: Which LED type uses just one GPIO pin and a resistor for a simple status light?
quiz.explanation: A single-color LED needs one GPIO pin plus a 220–470 Ω series resistor. It shows one fixed color and is perfect for power and status indicators.

quiz.question: Which LED type can show 16.7 million colors AND control each pixel independently?
quiz.explanation: NeoPixels deliver 24-bit color (16,777,216 colors) and each pixel in the chain is independently addressable — unlike an RGB LED, which shows one mixed color at a time.
